questions.question: Which access specifier makes a class member accessible only to the class's own member functions?
questions.explanation: The 'private' access specifier restricts access to a member so that only the class's own member functions (and friends) can access it. This enforces encapsulation.

questions.question: What is a constructor in a C++ class?
choices: A function that deletes an object
A special member function that is automatically called when an object is created
A function that copies one object to another
A function that returns the class type
questions.explanation: A constructor is a special member function with the same name as the class. It is automatically called when an object is created and is typically used to initialize member variables.

questions.question: When separating a class's specification from its implementation, which operator is used before the function name in the .cpp file?
questions.explanation: The scope resolution operator (::) ties a member function definition to its class. For example, void Rectangle::setWidth(double w) identifies setWidth as belonging to the Rectangle class.

questions.question: What character must appear at the end of a class declaration (after the closing brace)?
questions.explanation: A class declaration must end with a semicolon after the closing brace. Forgetting this semicolon is a common syntax error that often produces confusing compiler messages.

questions.question: A ___ is a special member function that is automatically called when an object is destroyed.
questions.explanation: A destructor has the same name as the class preceded by a tilde (~). It is automatically called when an object goes out of scope or is deleted, and is used to release resources.

questions.question: By default, all members of a C++ class are ___ if no access specifier is provided.
questions.explanation: Unlike structures (where members default to public), class members default to private access. You must explicitly use the 'public:' specifier to make members accessible outside the class.
